content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach consistently chose moves that matched the live read and timed them precisely. Turn 2 refuses to engage the 'selfish' verdict and goes straight to the want — exactly the right opening move. Turn 4 names the bid for verdict ('You're asking me to answer it for you') and then invites the want without the question attached — a clean find-the-want-behind-the-should move. When the client describes the cabin and then immediately tightens back into the selfish question (turn 5), the coach in turn 6 names the pattern ('You keep coming back to the question right when the want gets real') and asks about the sad noticing — staying with feeling before story. Turn 8 is a textbook hold: 'Stay with the sad for a second. Before the but.' The coach earns each next step; doesn't jump to compassion for the partner, doesn't moralize, doesn't issue a verdict. Turn 10 holds silence appropriately ('And you said it anyway') and asks what it's like to have it in the room — integration without rushing. Turn 12 surfaces the deeper question underneath the surface question at exactly the moment the client could receive it. Sequence integrity is intact: refuse-verdict → want → feeling (sad) → fear underneath. Hit rate is very high.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens by refusing the verdict and going straight to the want — correct first move for this concern."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the bid for verdict and invites the want without the question attached. Two clean moves stacked appropriately."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the pattern (client returning to the question when want gets real) and pivots to the feeling that just emerged. Reads-to-move match is precise."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Short, direct hold: 'Stay with the sad before the but.' Earns depth by not letting the client talk themselves out of the feeling."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Holds the moment after disclosure rather than rushing; gentle integration question lets the vulnerability land."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Surfaces the real question underneath at the moment the client is regulated enough to receive it. Good sequencing."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "linguistic-caveat"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["childhood-imprinting", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want"],
  "notes": "Conversation ended before childhood-imprinting (inherited 'selfish' label) could be touched, but the work was on track. State-the-want-not-the-action was implicitly attempted in turn 4."
}
